EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level trough moving into the Mid Atlantic and Northeast, skirted just north of central North Carolina on the afternoon of August 2nd. The trough passage was close enough to trigger scattered showers and thunderstorms during peak heating. Some of the storms became severe, producing large hail, damaging winds, and flash flooding across portions of the western Piedmont, the Sandhills, and the southern Coastal Plain of North Carolina.

An upper level trough moving into the Mid Atlantic and Northeast, skirted just north of central North Carolina on the afternoon of August 2nd. The trough passage was close enough to trigger scattered showers and thunderstorms during peak heating. Some of the storms became severe, producing large hail, damaging winds, and flash flooding across portions of the western Piedmont, the Sandhills, and the southern Coastal Plain of North Carolina.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Straight line winds downed a tree through a house along NC Highway 96 north just to the northwest of Emit.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Several severe storms affected western North Carolina during the afternoon and evening hours.
Local Atmosphere became strongly unstable during the afternoon and evening and severe convection erupted in this environment ahead of an outflow boundary.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Numerous trees were reported down across the western half of the county.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Numerous thunderstorms developed near the coast of eastern North Carolina during the late afternoon and evening hours ahead of a cold front. Unstable air ahead of the cold front combined with strong wind shear to produce rotating thunderstorms and several tornadoes were reported near the south coast in parts of Onslow...Jones and Carteret counties.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Public reported a brief tornado touchdown just north of Beaufort. Rated as an EF0 with no damage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Severe storms developed over the North Carolina mountains during the early afternoon hours. The storms progressed eastward during the afternoon and evening affecting much of western North Carolina.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Hail lasted for several minutes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Over the course of two day, several shortwave vorticity maxes rotated across the area as a closed upper low spun rotated through the region. Severe thunderstorms developed amidst steepening mid level lapse rates and peak afternoon heating. The severe storms resulted in mainly large hail, ranging from penny to golf ball size hail with an isolated flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Golf ball size hail was reported near the intersection of Highway 42 and Buckhorn Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level area of low pressure moved across the region ahead of a weak upstream cold front. These features combined with an unstable air mass to produce widespread strong to severe thunderstorms that produced not only large hail but also included some wind damage on June 22.

A cold front dropped southeast into eastern North Carolina during the morning of April 20 2008. A weak area of low pressure developed along the front over eastern North Carolina during the afternoon producing numerous showers and scattered thunderstorms. Some of the thunderstorms became severe producing hail and isolated tornadoes.

An upper level area of low pressure moved across eastern North Carolina during the afternoon of April 21 2008. Cold temperatures in the mid levels of the atmosphere were conducive for thunderstorms with many producing hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Golf ball size hail cover the ground around Pollocksville.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Numerous thunderstorms developed across central North Carolina when a cold front and associated shortwave trough moved through central North Carolina during strong daytime heating. The severe storms produced widespread reports of large hail with only scattered thunderstorm wind damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Nickel size hail was reported near Interstate 95 and Highway 70 in Smithfield.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Numerous thunderstorms developed across central North Carolina when a cold front and associated shortwave trough moved through central North Carolina during strong daytime heating. The severe storms produced widespread reports of large hail with only scattered thunderstorm wind damage.

A strong upper level low moving east-northeast across Kentucky and Tennessee, triggered several instances of severe damaging winds and two  tornadoes across the Triad region in the northwest piedmont of North Carolina.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: One tree was blown down onto Kermit Duncan Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered severe thunderstorms produced damaging winds, large hail and one tornado across portions of northeast North Carolina.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Golfball size hail was reported near Kelford.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered severe thunderstorms produced damaging winds and a tornado across portions of northeast North Carolina.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Numerous trees were downed off Highway 305.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The remnants of Tropical Storm Fay stalled just west of the Appalachian mountains, resulting in a prolonged, moist south to southeasterly flow over western North Carolina. During the early morning hours of Wed, August 27th, rainfall became especially heavy over the western North Carolina Piedmont as the southerly winds were lifted over a weak stationary front. Storm total rainfall in this area averaged 8 to 10 inches, with locally higher amounts, resulting in significant urban and stream flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Wilson Creek flooded Brown Mountain Beach Rd about 10 miles west of Lenoir, while the Johns River flooded a portion of Playmore Beach Rd in the same general area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front crossed eastern North Carolina during the late evening hours of May 20th. Thunderstorms became numerous across the area during the late afternoon and early evening hours ahead of the cold front. Some of the thunderstorms became severe producing numerous reports of large hail.
Thunderstorms formed during the afternoon hours.  One of these storms reached severe levels by producing winds that downed some trees.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: One tree was blown down onto a power line at Red Brush Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Supercell thunderstorms developed over the North Carolina foothills and Piedmont during the evening hours. Large hail, damaging winds and a few tornadoes were produced.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Reported in the Wilson Creek Gorge.
